title: Zygia ramiflora
taxonomy: Zygia ramiflora is a species of flowering plant in the legume family. It belongs to the genus Zygia and the subfamily Faboideae. This species is native to tropical Central and South America. Zygia ramiflora has a complex classification, and its full classification is Zygia ramiflora (Benth.) L. Rico. The classification includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Fabales.
uses: Zygia ramiflora is a plant species native to tropical regions, and while specific information on its medicinal uses is limited, it may offer potential benefits in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Zygia ramiflora include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
